revision.text:
胸肩神社(むなかたじんじゃ)は青森県弘前市品川町に鎮座する神社である。
=== 祭神 === 三女神 市杵島姫命(いちきしまひめのみこと)、湍津姫命(たぎつひめのみこと)、田心姫命(たぎりひめのみこと)。 道を守る神様で交通、安産、子授け、学問、商売、諸産業、水に深い関係のある神様。 水が湧いている所。川、池、海のそば。 辰巳(たつみ)歳生まれの守り神様。 ​ 九州福岡県「宗像大社(むなかたたいしゃ)」が本社以前は、弁財天(べんざいてん)（弁天様(べんてんさま)）を祀(まつ)っていたが、明治時代になり同じ水に深い関係のある胸肩の神様を祀る。今でも「弁天さま」と呼ばれている。 === 由緒 === ・大同２年（８０７年、今から１２０５年前）、坂上田村麿将軍(さかのうえたむらまろしようぐん)が大野という地に、信仰(しんこう)していた弁財天を祀り創建(そうけん)
・寛文２年（１６６２年）、現在地に移転。 ​ === 祭祀 === 毎年7月1日に例大祭、6月30日に宵宮祭執り行います。 ​宵宮祭では様々な神賑行事を行い、多くの参拝者で賑わいます。 === 交通=== ​・鉄道―JR弘前駅下車 徒歩15分 === 公式HP === https://www.muanakta.com/

revision.comment: Unamu がページ「[[Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/胸肩神社]]」を「[[胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)]]」に移動しました: url記述を除去。青森県内にはほかにも同名の神社があるため、区別のため所在自治体を括弧書き。
revision.text:
胸肩神社(むなかたじんじゃ)は青森県弘前市品川町に鎮座する神社である。
=== 祭神 === 三女神 市杵島姫命(いちきしまひめのみこと)、湍津姫命(たぎつひめのみこと)、田心姫命(たぎりひめのみこと)。 道を守る神様で交通、安産、子授け、学問、商売、諸産業、水に深い関係のある神様。 水が湧いている所。川、池、海のそば。 辰巳(たつみ)歳生まれの守り神様。 ​ 九州福岡県「宗像大社(むなかたたいしゃ)」が本社以前は、弁財天(べんざいてん)（弁天様(べんてんさま)）を祀(まつ)っていたが、明治時代になり同じ水に深い関係のある胸肩の神様を祀る。今でも「弁天さま」と呼ばれている。 === 由緒 === ・大同２年（８０７年、今から１２０５年前）、坂上田村麿将軍(さかのうえたむらまろしようぐん)が大野という地に、信仰(しんこう)していた弁財天を祀り創建(そうけん)
・寛文２年（１６６２年）、現在地に移転。 ​ === 祭祀 === 毎年7月1日に例大祭、6月30日に宵宮祭執り行います。 ​宵宮祭では様々な神賑行事を行い、多くの参拝者で賑わいます。 === 交通=== ​・鉄道―JR弘前駅下車 徒歩15分 === 公式HP === https://www.muanakta.com/

revision.text:
胸肩神社(むなかたじんじゃ)は青森県弘前市品川町に鎮座する神社である。
=== 祭神 === 三女神 市杵島姫命(いちきしまひめのみこと)、湍津姫命(たぎつひめのみこと)、田心姫命(たぎりひめのみこと)。 道を守る神様で交通、安産、子授け、学問、商売、諸産業、水に深い関係のある神様。 水が湧いている所。川、池、海のそば。 辰巳(たつみ)歳生まれの守り神様。 ​ 九州福岡県「宗像大社(むなかたたいしゃ)」が本社以前は、弁財天(べんざいてん)（弁天様(べんてんさま)）を祀(まつ)っていたが、明治時代になり同じ水に深い関係のある胸肩の神様を祀る。今でも「弁天さま」と呼ばれている。 === 由緒 === ・大同２年（８０７年、今から１２０５年前）、坂上田村麿将軍(さかのうえたむらまろしようぐん)が大野という地に、信仰(しんこう)していた弁財天を祀り創建(そうけん)
・寛文２年（１６６２年）、現在地に移転。 ​ === 祭祀 === 毎年7月1日に例大祭、6月30日に宵宮祭執り行います。 ​宵宮祭では様々な神賑行事を行い、多くの参拝者で賑わいます。 === 交通=== ​・鉄道―JR弘前駅下車 徒歩15分 === 公式HP === https://www.muanakta.com/

{{出典の明記|date=2019年9月}} {{神社 |名称 = 胸肩神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=弘前市}} |所在地 = [[青森県]][[弘前市]]品川町89 |ISO = JP-02 | 緯度度 = 40 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 40.5 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 28 |経度秒 = 41.0 |祭神 = {{ublist| [[市杵島姫命]] | [[湍津姫命]] | [[田心姫命]] }} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ublist| 宵宮祭 6月30日 | 例大祭 7月1日 }} |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aomori }} '''胸肩神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[青森県]][[弘前市]]品川町にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == ; 三女神 : [[市杵島姫命]] : [[湍津姫命]] : [[田心姫命]] ; 道を守る神 : 交通、安産、子授け、学問、商売、諸産業、他 ; 水に深い関係のある神 : 水が湧いている所、川、池、海のそば ; 辰巳歳生まれの守り神 : 福岡県[[宗像大社]]が本社以前は弁財天を祀っていたが、明治時代になり同じ水に深い関係のある胸肩の神を祀る。今でも「弁天さま」と呼ばれている。
== 由緒 == * [[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）、[[坂上田村麿]]将軍が大野という地に信仰していた弁財天を祀り創建。 * [[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）、現在地に移転。 ​ == 祭祀 == 毎年7月1日に例大祭、6月30日に宵宮祭が執り行われる。
宵宮祭では様々な神賑行事が行われる。
== 交通 == ; 鉄道 : [[弘前駅|JR弘前駅]]下車 徒歩15分
== 外部リンク == * {{公式サイト|https://www.muanakta.com/}}

{{出典の明記|date=2019年9月}} {{神社 |名称 = 胸肩神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=弘前市}} |所在地 = [[青森県]][[弘前市]]品川町89 |ISO = JP-02 | 緯度度 = 40 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 40.5 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 28 |経度秒 = 41.0 |祭神 = {{ublist| [[市杵島姫命]] | [[湍津姫命]] | [[田心姫命]] }} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ublist| 宵宮祭 6月30日 | 例大祭 7月1日 }} |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aomori }} '''胸肩神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[青森県]][[弘前市]]品川町にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == ; 三女神 : [[市杵島姫命]] : [[湍津姫命]] : [[田心姫命]] ; 道を守る神 : 交通、安産、子授け、学問、商売、諸産業、他 ; 水に深い関係のある神 : 水が湧いている所、川、池、海のそば ; 辰巳歳生まれの守り神 : 福岡県[[宗像大社]]が本社以前は弁財天を祀っていたが、明治時代になり同じ水に深い関係のある胸肩の神を祀る。「弁天さま」と呼ばれている。
== 由緒 == * [[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）、[[坂上田村麻呂]]将軍が大野という地に信仰していた弁財天を祀り創建。 * [[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）、現在地に移転。
== 祭祀 == 毎年7月1日に例大祭、6月30日に宵宮祭が執り行われる。
宵宮祭では様々な神賑行事が行われる。
== 交通 == ; 鉄道 : [[弘前駅|JR弘前駅]]下車 徒歩15分
== 外部リンク == * {{公式サイト|https://www.muanakta.com/}}
{{神道 横}} {{Pref-stub|pref=青森県}} {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) |image = {{Image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deities = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; Deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aomori Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deities = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deities = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aomori Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deities = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aomori Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deities = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aomori Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."

The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aomori Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.

; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]

'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.

; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.

{{Citation needed|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine (胸肩神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hirosaki City}} |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]] [[Hirosaki City]] Shinagawa-cho 89 |ISO = JP-02 |latitude_deg = 40 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 40.5 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 41.0 |deity = {{ublist| {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} | {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} | {{ill|Tachirahime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} }} |sacred_objects = |rank = |founded = {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE) |chief_priest = |main_hall = |alternative_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |major_festivals = {{ublist| Evening Festival June 30 | Grand Festival July 1 }} |rituals = |map = Japan Aomori }}
'''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hirosaki City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], Japan.
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Three Goddesses : {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} : {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}} : {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} ; Protector of Roads : Guardian of transportation, safe childbirth, fertility, learning, commerce, and industries. ; deity associated with water : Found near springs, rivers, ponds, and the sea. ; Guardian deity for those born in the year of the Dragon or Snake : Before [[Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] became the main shrine, the site enshrined Benzaiten. During the [[Meiji era]], the shrine began worshiping Munakata deities, similarly linked to water. Locally, these deities are still referred to as "Benten-sama."
== History ==
* In {{ill|Daido|ja|大同}} 2 (807 CE), General {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} founded the shrine, enshrining Benzaiten at a site called Ōno. * In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 2 (1662 CE), the shrine was relocated to its current location.
== Festivals ==
The Grand Festival is held annually on July 1, with an Evening Festival the day before on June 30.
The Evening Festival includes various ceremonial and entertainment events.
== Access ==
; Railway : 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hirosaki Station|ja|弘前駅}} on the JR Line.
== External Links == * [https://www.muanakta.com/ Official Website]
